title: Isolate untrusted PR code from secrets
description: Keep secret-bearing logic out of pull_request_target jobs that build fork code; split trusted and untrusted phases.
body: Use when a workflow must label, comment, or deploy previews for PRs from forks. Do not check out and execute the PR head in a pull_request_target job that has secrets in scope, because pull_request_target runs with the base repo's token and secrets against attacker-controlled code. Actions: do untrusted build/test under pull_request (no secrets) and gate privileged follow-up behind workflow_run or an environment with required reviewers; never run fork-supplied scripts with a secret in the env. Validation signals include no secrets referenced in any job that checks out PR head ref. Risk boundary: combining pull_request_target, PR-head checkout, and secrets is a critical exposure requiring a human gate.
